survey.domainSummary: A data pipeline that harvests artifacts, converts them to MP3, and manages a podcast feed.

entities.name: Artifact
entities.description: Represents a media file that needs processing.

properties.name: url
properties.description: URL where the artifact is located

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type of the artifact (e.g., audio, video)

relationships.description: Artifact is associated with an episode

validations: URL must be valid and accessible
Type must be one of the supported formats

scenarios.name: Process artifact
scenarios.given: An artifact with a valid URL
scenarios.then: The artifact is downloaded and converted to MP3 format
and: A temporary file is created for processing
The artifact is logged for tracking

entities.name: Episode
entities.description: Represents an episode in a podcast.
validations: Created_at must be a valid datetime

scenarios.name: Purge old episodes
scenarios.given: Episodes older than a certain threshold
scenarios.then: Old episodes are removed from the history

scenarios.name: Rebuild feed
scenarios.then: The podcast feed is regenerated
and: Episode media files are checked for existence
The feed is written to the specified path

entities.name: Media
entities.description: Represents media associated with an episode.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Size of the media file in bytes

validations: Path must be a valid file path
Size must be a positive integer

scenarios.name: Check media existence
scenarios.then: The media file is checked for existence
and: The media file size is verified

services.name: HarvesterService
services.purpose: Manages the lifecycle of podcast episodes, including fetching artifacts, processing them into MP3 files, maintaining a history of episodes, purging old episodes, and rebuilding the podcast feed.

operations.name: loadHistory
operations.description: Loads the history of processed episodes from a JSON file.

scenarios.name: History file exists
scenarios.then: History dictionary is returned

scenarios.name: History file does not exist
scenarios.given: History file does not exist at HISTORY_PATH
scenarios.then: Empty dictionary is returned

operations.name: saveHistory
operations.description: Saves the history of processed episodes to a JSON file.

scenarios.name: History file is saved successfully
scenarios.then: History is saved to HISTORY_PATH

operations.name: convertToMp3
operations.description: Converts a WAV file to an MP3 file using FFmpeg.

scenarios.name: Conversion succeeds
scenarios.given: Valid WAV file path and MP3 file path
scenarios.then: MP3 file is created at the specified path

scenarios.name: Conversion fails
scenarios.given: Invalid WAV file path or MP3 file path
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

operations.name: fetchArtifacts
operations.description: Fetches a list of artifacts from the API.

scenarios.name: Artifacts fetched successfully
scenarios.given: API is reachable and returns a list of artifacts
scenarios.then: List of artifacts is returned

scenarios.name: API is unreachable
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

operations.name: processArtifact
operations.description: Processes an artifact into an MP3 file and updates the history.

scenarios.name: Artifact processed successfully
scenarios.then: Artifact is processed into an MP3 file and history is updated

scenarios.name: Artifact processing fails
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

operations.name: purgeOldEpisodes
operations.description: Purges episodes older than RETENTION_DAYS from the history and filesystem.

scenarios.name: Old episodes purged successfully
scenarios.then: Old episodes are removed from history and filesystem

scenarios.name: No old episodes to purge
scenarios.given: History dictionary with no old episodes
scenarios.then: No changes are made

operations.name: rebuildFeed
operations.description: Rebuilds the podcast feed XML file from the history of episodes.

scenarios.name: Feed rebuilt successfully
scenarios.then: Podcast feed XML file is updated

scenarios.name: Feed rebuild fails
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

operations.name: runCycle
operations.description: Runs a complete cycle of fetching artifacts, processing them, purging old episodes, and rebuilding the feed.

scenarios.name: Cycle runs successfully
scenarios.then: Artifacts are fetched, processed, old episodes are purged, and feed is rebuilt

scenarios.name: Cycle fails
scenarios.given: Invalid history dictionary or API unreachable
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

operations.name: main
operations.description: Main entry point that loads history, runs a cycle, and saves history.

scenarios.name: Main runs successfully
scenarios.given: History file exists or can be created
scenarios.then: History is loaded, cycle is run, and history is saved

scenarios.name: Main fails
scenarios.given: History file cannot be loaded or saved
scenarios.then: Exception is raised

sideEffects: Executes FFmpeg subprocess for MP3 conversion

endpoints.purpose: Fetch a list of artifacts from the NotebookLM API.

scenarios.name: Fetch artifacts
scenarios.then: 200 OK with a list of artifacts in the response body

endpoints.purpose: Convert a WAV file to MP3 and save it to the episodes directory.

scenarios.name: Convert WAV to MP3
scenarios.then: 201 Created with the MP3 filename and creation date in the response body

endpoints.purpose: Generate an RSS feed from the history of episodes.

scenarios.name: Generate RSS feed
scenarios.then: 200 OK with the RSS feed in the response body

endpoints.purpose: Purge old episodes that are older than the retention period.

scenarios.name: Purge old episodes
scenarios.given: A history of episodes with some older than the retention period
scenarios.then: 200 OK with a list of purged episodes in the response body

architecture.systemPurpose: OpenSpec is a data pipeline that harvests podcast episodes from a NotebookLM API, converts them to MP3 format, and manages a podcast feed. It automates the process of fetching, processing, and maintaining podcast episodes, ensuring a seamless user experience.
architecture.architectureStyle: Monolithic architecture: The system is a single, unified unit with all components tightly coupled. This pattern was chosen for simplicity and ease of deployment, given the relatively small scale of the project.
architecture.dataFlow: HTTP request → route handler → service → entity operations → persistence; async processing of artifacts to MP3 via HarvesterService
architecture.securityModel: No explicit authentication/authorization mechanism is evident from the provided analysis. This is a potential area for improvement.
keyDecisions: Monolithic architecture chosen for simplicity and ease of deployment
HarvesterService centralizes the business logic for managing podcast episodes
Entities represent the data model and are used throughout the system
